name: ES-9
description: Class-compliant 16-in / 16-out USB-C audio interface with 14 DC-coupled 3.5mm inputs, 8 DC-coupled 3.5mm outputs, balanced 1/4" mains, headphones, S/PDIF, on-board 8x8 crosspoint mixer with EQ, and ES-5 / MIDI expansion headers.

controls.name: HEADPHONE VOLUME
controls.description: Analog volume knob for the 1/4" headphone output. Push-button: hold ~1 s to swap between the two configuration slots stored in flash (hosted / standalone).
controls.detail: LED C lit = hosted slot last loaded · LED D lit = standalone slot last loaded

inputs.name: IN 1–14
inputs.description: Fourteen 3.5mm TS analog inputs, DC-coupled. Accept audio or CV. Illuminated jacks: red = positive V · blue = negative V · purple = audio. Per-pair switchable DC-blocking filters (enable for audio, disable for CV).

inputs.name: S/PDIF IN (TOSlink)
inputs.description: Optical S/PDIF digital input. Appears as DAW channels 15/16 by default. Disabled when the configuration tool enables a second mixer instead.

inputs.name: USB-C
inputs.description: Class-compliant USB 2.0 audio / MIDI. macOS, iOS, Linux driverless; Windows driver available. Host powers nothing — module runs on Eurorack rails. LED B lights on successful host connection.

inputs.name: GT1 header (rear)
inputs.description: 10-pin ribbon to an ES-5 expansion. Adds 8 digital outputs driven by DAW channels 7–8 by default.

inputs.name: GT2 header (rear)
inputs.description: 4-pin MIDI breakout header. Pins: 1 OUT-4, 2 OUT-5, 3 IN-5, 4 IN-4. Uses 3.3 V low-voltage-signaling (MMA LVS-compliant). LED C = MIDI in from host to breakout · LED D = breakout to host.

inputs.name: JP1 jumper (rear)
inputs.description: Fit jumper to force DFU firmware-update boot when USB updater cannot reach runtime.

outputs.name: MAIN OUT L / R
outputs.description: Pair of balanced 1/4" TRS outputs, AC-coupled. Driven by mixer 1 outputs 1–2 by default (= DAW 1/2). For monitors or desk.

outputs.name: HEADPHONE OUT
outputs.description: 1/4" TRS stereo headphone jack, DC-coupled. Driven by mixer 1 outputs 3–4 by default (blend of DAW 1/2 and 3/4).

outputs.name: OUT 1–8
outputs.description: Eight 3.5mm TS analog outputs, DC-coupled. Usable for audio or CV. Default routing: mixer outputs 5–8 to OUT 1–4, DAW channels 9–16 to OUT 1–8 (fully remappable).

outputs.name: S/PDIF OUT (TOSlink)
outputs.description: Optical S/PDIF digital output. Fed by DAW channels 5/6 by default.

behaviors.name: Hosted vs standalone configurations
behaviors.description: Two independent routings live in flash. On first USB handshake the module loads the hosted slot; without USB it boots the standalone slot. Hold the knob for a second to manually swap slots. LEDs C and D indicate the active slot. Makes live recovery from USB dropouts graceful.

behaviors.name: On-board 8×8 crosspoint mixer
behaviors.description: Forms eight mono mixes from any eight of: hardware inputs 1–14, internal buses 1–16, USB outputs 1–16, mixer-2 outputs 1–8, or the S/PDIF-processor outputs. Runs on-chip, so mixing keeps working when no host is connected.

behaviors.name: S/PDIF or Mixer 2 mutex
behaviors.description: The third DSP block is either the S/PDIF I/O or a second 8×8 mixer — not both. Enabling mixer 2 disables TOSlink; DAW channels 15/16 are then free for an extra mixer bus feeding the hardware.

behaviors.name: Per-pair input DC blocking
behaviors.trigger: Input DC Blocking check-boxes in Configuration Tool
behaviors.description: ADCs have switchable DC-blocking filters organised in pairs (1/2, 3/8, 4/5, 6/7, 9/10, 11/12, 13/14). Enable for audio to kill DC offsets and pops; disable for CV capture. A pair is either on or off — no per-channel override.

behaviors.name: Up to 4-band per-input EQ
behaviors.description: Each of the eight mixer-1 inputs can have up to four bands of EQ: LP/HP 1st or 2nd order, low shelf, high shelf, peak, or phase-invert. Each band sets frequency, Q and gain. DSP usage is shown in the tool; cap at 100 %.

behaviors.name: Mix smoothing for live MIDI control
behaviors.trigger: Mix Smoothing check-boxes · MIDI CC 0–63 into mixer
behaviors.description: Interpolates and slew-limits macro-mix values (7-bit, MIDI-compatible) into the underlying 16-bit raw mix. Hides the stair-stepping when a MIDI controller rides a fader. Smoothing shares DSP budget with EQ.

behaviors.name: MIDI CC mixer control
behaviors.trigger: Mixer MIDI channels set in Options · MIDI CC
behaviors.description: Mix faders are mapped CC 0–63 counting by channel first then mixer (mix 1 ch 1 = CC 0, mix 1 ch 8 = CC 7, mix 2 ch 1 = CC 8…). For stereo-linked channels the lower-numbered CC controls gain and the higher-numbered CC becomes pan. USB and DIN channels can be set independently or disabled.

behaviors.name: Output DC offsets
behaviors.trigger: Output DC offsets section in Configuration Tool
behaviors.description: Each of OUT 1–8 accepts a trim value that adds a steady DC offset to compensate for downstream miscalibration. Implemented internally via the mixer hardware — a mixer must be routed to that output for the offset to take effect.

behaviors.name: ES-5 expansion
behaviors.description: Adds the ES-5's eight digital outputs and further ESX-8xx expansion chains without consuming 3.5mm outputs. ES-5 is driven by DAW channels 7/8 by default; remap via routing. Used for gates, triggers, clocks, and third-party class-compliant CV expansions.

behaviors.name: SysEx external control
behaviors.trigger: Expert Sleepers SysEx over USB or DIN
behaviors.description: Manufacturer ID 00H 21H 27H, device byte 19H. Supports configuration dump (09H) / request (23H), save (24H) / restore (25H) / reset (26H) to either flash slot, set mix (60H–6FH), set filter (39H), set inputs / outputs routing (40H–43H / 50H–53H), version query (22H). Enables rack-side automation without the GUI tool.

behaviors.name: MIDI Thru standalone
behaviors.description: When enabled and running standalone, the DIN MIDI OUT becomes a hardware thru of DIN MIDI IN. Ignored when a USB host is connected. Lets the breakout keep merging/forwarding external gear in unhosted rigs.

behaviors.name: Illuminated jacks as visual meter
behaviors.description: Each 3.5mm socket has a bicolor LED ring: red = positive voltage, blue = negative voltage, purple = audio (rapid alternation). Gives instant visual confirmation of polarity and level without a dedicated meter — diagnostic gold when troubleshooting routing.

patchIdeas.name: DAW multitrack recording of modular
patchIdeas.patch: enable DC-blocking on input pairs used for audio · route each module's audio out to IN 1–14 · set DAW to 16 tracks mapped to inputs 1–14 + S/PDIF 15/16 · arm and record · 14 simultaneous modular channels + optional stereo S/PDIF from an outboard converter
patchIdeas.diagram:
ES-9:
* Input DC Blocking: all on

Drums:

Bass:

Synth:

FX:

- Drums (Out) -> ES-9 (IN 1)
- Bass (Out) -> ES-9 (IN 2)
- Synth (L) -> ES-9 (IN 3)
- Synth (R) -> ES-9 (IN 4)
- FX (L) -> ES-9 (IN 5)
- FX (R) -> ES-9 (IN 6)
- ES-9 (USB-C) -> Output (DAW)

patchIdeas.name: Plug-in FX loop via send / return
patchIdeas.patch: route two modular outs into IN 13/14 (DC block on) · in the DAW insert a reverb/compressor plug-in on those inputs · route the DAW plug-in output back through USB channels 15/16 · ES-9 maps USB 15/16 to OUT 7/8 via the mixer · near-zero-latency plug-in as a modular insert
patchIdeas.diagram:
ES-9:
* Mixer 1: USB 15 -> OUT 7, USB 16 -> OUT 8

Source:

DAW:

- Source (L) -> ES-9 (IN 13)
- Source (R) -> ES-9 (IN 14)
- ES-9 (Out USB-C) -> DAW (Plugin In)
- DAW (Plugin Out) -> ES-9 (In USB-C)
- ES-9 (OUT 7) -> Mixer (L)
- ES-9 (OUT 8) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Silent Way / CV Tools CV sequencing
patchIdeas.patch: disable DC-blocking on IN pairs used for CV capture (not needed for outputs) · in the DAW load Silent Way / Bitwig CV Tools · send 1V/oct to ES-9 USB channels 9–16 which drive OUT 1–8 DC-coupled · any modular VCO/filter controlled from the DAW in perfect sync
patchIdeas.diagram:
ES-9:
* Outputs: DC-coupled

DAW:

VCO:

Filter:

- DAW (Pitch) -> ES-9 (USB-C)
- ES-9 (OUT 1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- ES-9 (OUT 2) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- ES-9 (OUT 3) g> VCO (Gate)
- VCO (Out) -> Output (L)

patchIdeas.name: Standalone mixer (no laptop)
patchIdeas.patch: Reset to defaults suitable for standalone · Save to Flash (standalone) · mixer 1 inputs set to hardware IN 1–8 · mixer 1 out 1/2 → MAIN OUT · out 3/4 → HEADPHONES · ES-9 now functions as an 8-in stereo mixer with EQ and headphones, no USB host
patchIdeas.diagram:
ES-9:
* Reset: defaults (standalone)
* Mixer 1 inputs: IN 1-8
* Mix 1/2: MAIN OUT
* Mix 3/4: HEADPHONES

Source1:

Source2:

- Source1 (Out) -> ES-9 (IN 1)
- Source2 (Out) -> ES-9 (IN 2)
- ES-9 (MAIN OUT L) -> Output (Monitor L)
- ES-9 (MAIN OUT R) -> Output (Monitor R)
- ES-9 (HEADPHONE OUT) -> Output (Phones)

patchIdeas.name: ES-5 gates + analog CV hybrid
patchIdeas.patch: connect ES-5 via GT1 ribbon · DAW channels 7/8 drive the ES-5 (eight gate outs) · DAW channels 9–16 drive OUT 1–8 for analog CV / audio · one module handles bit-accurate gates and continuous CV from one plug-in instance
patchIdeas.diagram:
ES-9:
* ES-5 expander: connected

DAW:

Drum:

VCO:

- DAW (Pitch) -> ES-9 (USB-C)
- ES-9 (OUT 1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- ES-9 (ES-5-1) t> Drum (Trig) // DAW ch 7/8
- VCO (Out) -> Output (L)

patchIdeas.name: Live MIDI-controller mixing
patchIdeas.patch: Options · set USB Mixer MIDI channel to 1 · enable Mix Smoothing on the mixes you will ride · map CC 0–7 on a USB MIDI controller to mixer 1 faders · performative level / mute control of eight modular channels with slew-limited smooth transitions
patchIdeas.diagram:
ES-9:
* Mixer MIDI channel (USB): 1
* Mix Smoothing: Mix 1 on

Controller:

Monitor:

- Controller (USB) -> ES-9 (USB-C) // CC 0-7
- ES-9 (MAIN OUT L) -> Monitor (L)
- ES-9 (MAIN OUT R) -> Monitor (R)
